executive_summary: The AS-REP roasting attack using Rubeus was successful on the target, allowing the attacker to harvest credentials. This compromises the security of the domain-joined Windows system.

findings.title: AS-REP Roasting Attack Succeeded — Credential Harvest Possible
findings.detail: The Rubeus tool was used to perform an AS-REP roasting attack, which successfully harvested credentials from the domain-joined Windows system. This allows the attacker to potentially obtain plaintext passwords or NTLM hashes.
findings.recommendation: Configure Kerberos pre-authentication to be required for all user accounts. Enable and enforce strong password policies and consider implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA) for critical accounts.

key_observations: The Rubeus tool successfully performed an AS-REP roasting attack, indicating that some user accounts are vulnerable to this attack.
attack_path: Attacker ran Rubeus asreproast on tgt-1 (exit 0) → output showed successful credential harvesting → can now pivot to cracking the harvested hashes or using them for further attacks.
